questions.q: On what date is Valentine's Day celebrated each year?
Which ancient Roman god of love, typically shown with a bow and arrows, is a key symbol of the holiday?
Which flower is most traditionally given on Valentine's Day?
Which treat, often boxed, is a classic Valentine's gift along with flowers?
Small, pastel-colored, heart-shaped candies stamped with messages like 'BE MINE' are called what?
The symbol for 'a kiss' at the end of a letter — traditionally an X — originally represented what?
What warm, fuzzy brain hormone released during hugging and bonding is nicknamed 'the love hormone'?
In which Italian city is the legendary home of Romeo and Juliet located?
In Greek mythology, what is the name of Cupid, the god of love?
Which Roman emperor ordered the execution of St. Valentine?
What do the letters in the phrase 'XOXO' stand for?
Approximately how many heart-shaped boxes of chocolates are sold on Valentine's Day in the United States?
Which animal was favored by Aphrodite, the ancient goddess of love, for its monogamous nature?
In which country is Valentine's Day alternatively known as 'Friend's Day'?
What color of roses is traditionally given to symbolize romantic love?
In Roman mythology, who is the mother of Cupid?
What iconic landmark in New York illuminates a heart symbol every Valentine's Day?
In medieval times, which internal organ was believed to be the source of love?
Who wrote the famous line 'O Romeo, Romeo! Wherefore art thou Romeo?'
What gift did the Domino's Pizza founder give to his future wife on their first Valentine's Day?
What is the name given by florists to a single red rose surrounded by baby's breath?
In which month is Valentine's Day celebrated?
What is the Roman goddess of love, after whom the planet Venus is named?
Which U.S. state is the largest producer of roses sold on Valentine's Day?
What does the X represent when written at the end of a letter as a sign-off?
In Finland, Valentine's Day is known by what alternative name?
Which American town — named for the holiday — receives millions of letters to re-mail with a special February 14 postmark?
choices: All of the above offer similar programs

questions.q: Esther Howland, called the 'Mother of the American Valentine,' helped popularize what specific kind of Valentine product?
Pope Gelasius I is traditionally credited with designating February 14 as Saint Valentine's Day in what century?
Which English poet's 14th-century poem 'Parlement of Foules' is one of the earliest references linking Valentine's Day to romantic love?
In medieval Europe, women believed that eating which type of food on Valentine's Eve would cause them to dream of their future husband?
The oldest known Valentine's Day letter in existence was written in 1415 by a prisoner held in which fortress?
In ancient Roman tradition, the Lupercalia festival that influenced Valentine's Day customs was primarily celebrated to honor which goddess?
In Japanese Valentine's Day tradition, what specific type of chocolate do women typically give to men they do not have romantic feelings for?
The neurotransmitter phenylethylamine, often called 'the love chemical,' is naturally produced in the brain and is also found in abundance in which common substance?
Which U.S. President's wife, Edith Kermit Carow Roosevelt, was born on Valentine's Day?
The St. Valentine's Day Massacre of 1929 in Chicago resulted in the deaths of how many people in a single incident?
In Shakespeare's 'The Two Gentlemen of Verona,' which character leaves Milan to pursue a lady and become Valentine's rival?
The tradition of giving conversation hearts on Valentine's Day originated with the production of lozenges by which 19th-century company?
Which saint, whose feast day is celebrated on February 14, was martyred under the Roman Emperor Claudius II Gothicus?
The Greek goddess Aphrodite was said to have emerged from the sea foam generated by the castration of which god?
California produces approximately what percentage of all roses grown in the United States for Valentine's Day?
In Japanese folklore, what celestial event prevents two star-crossed lovers, Orihime and Hikoboshi, from meeting except once per year?
Which writer of epistolary novels created the character Pamela, whose virtue and constancy influenced 18th-century romantic literature and Valentine's Day ideals?
The heart symbol used universally to represent love was standardized in artistic and commercial usage beginning in which century?
In South Korea, the tradition of women giving gifts to men on Valentine's Day is reversed on a different holiday called what?
Which ancient Roman holiday, celebrated in February, combined elements of purification and fertility that influenced the modern Valentine's Day customs?
In which year did the Duke of Orleans compose the oldest-known written Valentine's Day message while imprisoned in the Tower of London?
In Roman mythology, which mortal princess did Cupid famously fall in love with, creating one of antiquity's greatest love stories?
What occupational group, aside from lovers, regards St. Valentine as their patron saint?
Which Shakespeare play features the characters Valentine and Proteus as the central protagonists in a tale of friendship and romance?
In Victorian era tradition, what was considered bad luck regarding Valentine's Day correspondence?
choices: Signing your name on the Valentine
Delivering the card on February 14th itself
Using red wax to seal the envelope

questions.q: What confectionery item, originally manufactured on equipment designed for medicinal lozenges, became a quintessential Valentine's Day candy with conversation phrases printed on it?
What color rose is traditionally given to express friendship (not romance) on Valentine's Day?
In Japan, who traditionally gives chocolates to whom on Valentine's Day?
A 1929 Chicago gangland mass killing is known as what?
Which 1860s British chocolate maker first marketed heart-shaped boxes of chocolates for Valentine's Day?
Which Shakespeare tragedy, set in Verona, has become closely associated with Valentine's Day as the ultimate love story?
Which U.S. president's mother and wife both died on Valentine's Day (the same day, in 1884)?
What Roman goddess's name is connected to the phrase 'wearing your heart on your sleeve'?
In Shakespeare's 'The Two Gentlemen of Verona,' how many characters share the name Valentine or Proteus as protagonists?
Who is credited with inventing the modern concept of speed dating?
Which animal does the Bronx Zoo permit visitors to name after their romantic partner on Valentine's Day?
In ancient Greek mythology, what natural phenomenon was Aphrodite said to have been born from?
During the Victorian era, what derogatory name was given to mean-spirited Valentine's Day cards designed to mock or insult the recipient?
In Irish folklore, what household item was traditionally carved with a heart as a Valentine's Day gift?
